L1.area: SIP message response construction and header processing

L2.description: A nil pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the SIP response creation logic. When a malformed SIP request is received without a required header, message parsing succeeds but the response construction code unconditionally accesses fields on the header object without checking for nil. A remote unauthenticated attacker can send a single crafted SIP request to crash the application, resulting in a complete denial of service.

L3.area: SIP message response construction and header processing
L3.description: A nil pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the SIP response creation logic. When a malformed SIP request is received without a required header, message parsing succeeds but the response construction code unconditionally accesses fields on the header object without checking for nil. A remote unauthenticated attacker can send a single crafted SIP request to crash the application, resulting in a complete denial of service.

ground_truth.reason: The NewResponseFromRequest function dereferences the To header without a nil check, causing a panic when a SIP request without a To header is processed into a response.
